DISCOVERY 302, MOTION TO DISMISS FOR FAILURE TO COMPLY WITH DISCOVERY ORDER AND OR IN THE ALTERNATIVE MOTION TO COMPEL FURTHER DISCOVERY RESPONSES AND REQUEST FOR MONETARY SANCTIONS SET ON NOV-14-2014, IN DEPT. 302. DEFENDANT'S MOTION TO COMPEL IS GRANTED. PLAINTIFF SHALL PROVIDE FULL RESPONSES TO ALL OUTSTANDING DISCOVERY (WITHOUT ANY OBJECTIONS) BY DECEMBER 1, 2014 AND PAY THE OUTSTANDING SANCTIONS AND ADDITIONAL SANCTIONS OF $100 BY THE SAME DATE. FAILURE TO COMPLY WITH THIS ORDER MAY RESULT IN TERMINATING SANCTIONS AND DISMISSAL OF THE CASE UPON FURTHER MOTION BY DEFENDANT. JUDGE PRO TEM: DAVID MCDONALD; CLERK: FELICIA GREEN; NOT REPORTED; DEPT. 302.

DISCOVERY 302, DEFENDANT BEAUTY SUPPLY WAREHOUSE'S MOTION FOR TERMINATING SANCTIONS FOR FAILURE TO COMPLY WITH THREE DISCOVERY ORDERS AND REQUEST FOR MONETARY SANCTIONS IS GRANTED. NO OPPOSITION FILED. THE COMPLAINT IS DISMISSED. PLAINTIFF TO PAY PAST DUE AND CURRENT SANCTIONS TOTALING $935. ORDER SIGNED. JUDGE PRO TEM: THOMAS COHEN. CLERK: ROSIE NOGUERA. NOT REPORTED. (302/JPT)

LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFF CONSTANCE HARRIS' MOTION TO RECONSIDER IS DENIED. PLAINTIFF FAILED TO COMPLY WITH THE REQUIREMENTS OF CCP 1008. PLAINTIFF DOES NOT PRESENT ANY NEW FACTS, LAW, OR CIRCUMSTANCES TO WARRANT RECONSIDERATION. PLAINTIFF DID NOT OPPOSE THE MOTION FOR TERMINATING SANCTIONS OR CONTEST THE TENTATIVE RULING. PLAINTIFF WAS PROPERLY DEEMED TO HAVE STIPULATED TO THE JUDGE PRO TEM HEARING THE MATTER BECAUSE SHE DID NOT RAISE A TIMELY OBJECTION. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Notice Of Motion And Motion To Reconsider
Off Calendar Aug-28-2015 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - CONSTANCE HARRIS, TEL. NO. 1-415-449-2272, PLAINTIFF APPEARING IN PRO PER;
ATTY. TINA W. LU OF THE LAW OFFICES OF JOHN A. BIARD, TEL. NO. 1-925-945-4003 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT.

RULING - A R G U E D; The Court adopted its tentative ruling. PLAINTIFF CONSTANCE HARRIS' Motion To Reconsider is denied. Plaintiff failed to comply with the requirements of CCP 1008. Plaintiff does not present any new facts, law, or circumstances to warrant reconsideration. Plaintiff did not oppose the motion for terminating sanctions or contest the tentative ruling. Plaintiff was properly deemed to have stipulated to the Judge Pro Tem hearing the matter because she did not raise a timely objection. Order signed in open court. Judge: Suzanne Bolanos; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter : Melanie Gheno, CSR # 7489

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Terminating Sanctions For Failure To Comply With Three Discovery Orders And Request For Monetary Sanctions
Off Calendar Jun-10-2015 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Tina Lu of Law Offices of John Biard for Defendant.

The Court adopted the tentative ruling. DEFENDANT BEAUTY SUPPLY WAREHOUSE'S Motion For Terminating Sanctions For Failure To Comply With Three Discovery Orders And Request For Monetary Sanctions is granted. No opposition filed. The complaint is dismissed. Plaintiff to pay past due and current sanctions totaling $935. Order signed.

Judge Pro Tem: Thomas Cohen. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Not reported. (302/JPT)

Constance Harris appeared after the order had been signed. The Court informed Ms. Harris that the tentative ruling stands, that the order had already been signed and noted that she failed to advise the Court and opposing Counsel of her intent to contest the tentative ruling.

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Dismiss For Failure To Comply With Discovery Order And Or In The Alternative Motion To Compel Further Discovery Responses And Request For Monetary Sanctions
Off Calendar Nov-14-2014 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances.

RULING: The Court adopts its tentative ruling on DEFENDANT BEAUTY SUPPLY WAREHOUSE Motion To Dismiss For Failure To Comply With Discovery Order And Or In The Alternative Motion To Compel Further Discovery Responses And Request For Monetary Sanctions. Defendant's motion to compel is granted. Plaintiff shall provide full responses to all outstanding discovery (without any objections) by December 1, 2014 and pay the outstanding sanctions and additional sanctions of $100 by the same date. Failure to comply with this Order may result in terminating sanctions and dismissal of the case upon further motion by Defendant. Judge Pro Tem: David McDonald; Clerk: Felicia Green; Not reported; Dept. 302.
